title: Is Peloton Broken, or Is It About to Make a Comeback?
text: Peloton Turnaround Analysis and Investment Thesis. Core Question: Following Q3 2026 earnings, investors are asking whether Peloton's recent improvements signal a sustainable turnaround or merely a temporary bounce. Evidence of Turnaround: Strong profitability metrics including adjusted EBITDA up 41% YoY and free cash flow up 59% YoY support the turnaround narrative. The company's first year-over-year revenue growth in years marks an important inflection point. Challenges Remain: Connected fitness subscriptions continue to decline 7.6% year-over-year, suggesting the legacy hardware business is mature and potentially declining. Structural Concerns: The decline in hardware subscriber growth raises questions about the long-term viability of the core business model. New Revenue Streams: The Spotify partnership and commercial expansion represent new growth vectors that could offset hardware decline. Strategic Initiatives: CEO Peter Stern's emphasis on strategic optionality and debt reduction provides flexibility for pursuing promising opportunities. Investor Perspective: The market's 12% surge in pre-market trading on Q3 earnings suggests investors believe the turnaround has merit, but full execution remains uncertain.
